assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Regular channel will be force-upgraded to the next minor version (1.31) when 1.30 hits EoS
assertions.reason: Response clearly states that Regular channel clusters will undergo 'Automatic upgrade to 1.31 (next supported minor version)' with both control plane AND node pools upgraded automatically when 1.30 hits EoS

assertions.expectation: Explains that clusters on Extended channel get up to 24 months of support, so 1.30 EoS is significantly later — they have more time
assertions.reason: Response provides specific timeline showing Extended channel gets extended support until May 28, 2026 (12 months after standard EoS), clearly explaining they have more time and continue running 1.30 with continued support

assertions.expectation: Explains that legacy 'No channel' clusters: control plane EoS minor versions are auto-upgraded to next supported minor, AND EoS node pools are force-upgraded even when 'no auto-upgrade' is configured
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states for 'No channel' clusters: 'Control plane: Auto-upgraded to 1.31' and 'Node pools: Auto-upgraded to 1.31 even if no auto-upgrade is configured'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that a 'no upgrades' maintenance exclusion (30 days) can temporarily defer even EoS force-upgrades
assertions.reason: Response includes specific section on 'Temporary Deferral' with exact gcloud command for 30-day 'no upgrades' exclusion and explicitly states it is 'honored even past EoS'

assertions.expectation: Recommends proactive upgrade planning rather than waiting for EoS enforcement
assertions.reason: Response prominently recommends 'Option 1: Proactive Manual Upgrade (Recommended)' and provides detailed timeline suggesting manual upgrades in Q1 2025 before forced EoS, emphasizing benefits of full control over timing

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging as a planning tool
assertions.reason: Response does not mention GKE EoS notifications via Cloud Logging at all - this monitoring/alerting capability is not discussed as a planning tool

assertions.expectation: Mentions the deprecation insights dashboard for checking API compatibility before the forced upgrade
assertions.reason: Response mentions checking deprecated API usage with kubectl command but does not specifically mention the deprecation insights dashboard as a tool for API compatibility checking

assertions.expectation: Warns that 'snowflaking' (manually freezing versions) should be avoided — it creates security and reliability risks
assertions.reason: Response does not use the term 'snowflaking' or explicitly warn against manually freezing versions. While it mentions security debt with temporary deferrals, it doesn't address the broader concept of version freezing risks
